question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query ANY account's full token allowance list (owner, spender, token_id, amount, amount_granted, timestamp) by simply supplying any valid :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress in the path with no authentication header, because getAccountTokenAllowances() performs no caller-identity check before calling EntityService.getEncodedId() and TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances(), causing complete disclosure of all approved spenders and amounts for any account on the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all token allowances granted by a victim account (owner) to every spender by iterating through paginated results via the next link returned in getPaginationLink(), with no credential required, allowing the attacker to map the full authorization graph of the victim and craft targeted unauthorized token transfer attempts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user determine which spender accounts have been granted token allowances by a victim owner by querying /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens with no authentication, and then use that spender list to impersonate or front-run authorized spenders in downstream transaction submission, effectively authorizing token movements without the owner's knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the intended owner-only visibility of token allowances by supplying a victim's EVM address as the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to resolve it to the victim's internal account ID and returning all allowance records without any check that the requester is the account owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a victim's account alias as the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter to EntityService.getEncodedId(), which resolves it via getAccountIdFromAlias() without verifying the caller's identity, thereby exposing all token allowances (spender, token_id, amount) of the victim account without any authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with spender.id=eq:<victim_spender> and token.id=eq:<target_token> to confirm whether a specific allowance exists between a victim owner and a victim spender for a specific token, leaking binary authorization state (allowance exists/does not exist) without any authentication, enabling targeted exploitation of known allowance relationships?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user use the spender.id range filters (gte/lte) in extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to enumerate all spender accounts that have been granted token allowances by a victim owner, by binary-searching the spender ID space with no authentication, revealing the complete set of authorized spenders without the owner's consent?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token.id range filters (gte/lte) in extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() combined with a known spender.id to enumerate all tokens for which a specific spender has been granted allowances by a victim owner, leaking the full token authorization scope of that spender without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user read the amount_granted field returned by TokenAllowanceViewModel for any account's token allowances without authentication, revealing the original approved amount that was signed by the owner, which an attacker can use to determine the maximum value of tokens they could attempt to transfer if they compromise the spender account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user read the timestamp.from field in the TokenAllowanceViewModel response to determine exactly when a token allowance was granted by the owner, enabling the attacker to correlate allowance creation with on-chain transactions and identify the specific transaction that authorized the allowance, without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens for a high-value account (e.g., exchange, treasury) with no authentication, retrieve the full list of token allowances including spender IDs and amounts, and use this information to identify high-value allowances that can be exploited if the spender account is compromised, without the owner ever being notified?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any rate limiting in getAccountTokenAllowances() to perform high-frequency enumeration of token allowances across thousands of accounts, building a comprehensive map of all token authorization relationships on the network, which can be used to identify targets for unauthorized token transfer attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user confirm whether a specific account has revoked a token allowance (amount drops to 0, filtered by amountCondition amount > 0) by querying the endpoint and observing the absence of a previously known allowance, leaking the revocation event without authentication and allowing the attacker to time attacks before revocation is processed?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination next link generated by getPaginationLink() to traverse all pages of token allowances for any account without authentication, where each page reveals additional spender/token/amount combinations, effectively bypassing any intended access control through paginated data harvesting?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getPaginationLink()] Can an unprivileged external user observe the spender and token_id values embedded in the next pagination link generated by getPaginationLink() (e.g., spender.id=gt:X&token.id=gt:Y) to infer the last allowance record returned, leaking internal account IDs and token IDs even before following the link, without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a shard.realm.num format account ID for any account in getAccountTokenAllowances() and receive all token allowances for that account without authentication, because the controller performs no ownership verification between the requester and the :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the token.id filter in acceptedTokenAllowanceParameters to probe whether a specific token has any allowances granted by a victim account, by querying with token.id=eq:<target_token_id> and a known spender.id, confirming or denying the existence of a specific authorization without any authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a request with limit set to the maximum allowed value in extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to retrieve the largest possible batch of token allowances in a single request for any account, maximizing data exfiltration per request without authentication, enabling efficient bulk harvesting of authorization data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user determine the exact remaining token allowance (amount field) for any spender/token pair by querying the endpoint without authentication, and use this information to calculate the maximum value of tokens that can be transferred by the spender without triggering an insufficient-allowance error, enabling precision attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user monitor changes in the amount field of a specific token allowance over time by repeatedly querying the endpoint without authentication, inferring the spending behavior of the authorized spender and timing an attack to exploit the allowance before it is fully consumed or revoked?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the order=desc parameter in acceptedTokenAllowanceParameters to retrieve the most recently granted token allowances first for any account without authentication, enabling targeted surveillance of newly created allowances that may represent high-value or time-sensitive authorizations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user query the endpoint for a smart contract account's token allowances without authentication, revealing which contracts have been granted spending rights over which tokens, enabling the attacker to identify vulnerable contract-to-contract allowance relationships that could be exploited through reentrancy or other contract-level attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getAccountTokenAllowances() does not verify whether the resolved accountId corresponds to an actual account (unlike tokenController.js which calls EntityService.isValidAccount()), allowing queries for non-existent or deleted accounts to return empty results without error, enabling silent enumeration of account existence?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the EVM address resolution path in EntityService.getEncodedId() (via getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()) to map EVM addresses to internal Hedera account IDs by observing whether the endpoint returns allowances or a not-found error, creating an oracle for EVM-address-to-account-ID mapping without authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the alias resolution path in EntityService.getEncodedId() (via getAccountIdFromAlias()) to confirm whether a given alias corresponds to a valid account by observing whether the endpoint returns allowances or throws a NotFoundError, creating an alias-validity oracle without authentication?
